message:
fcp-core/capability: reject non-Text INSTANCE_ID claim instead of silently bypassing instance binding

`CapabilityVerifier::verify` step 3.5 ("instance binding") was using a
nested `if let` that silently fell through whenever the INSTANCE_ID
claim was present but not a CBOR Text:

    if let Some(inst_val) = claims.get(fcp2_claims::INSTANCE_ID) {
        if let Some(inst_str) = inst_val.as_text() {
            if inst_str != self.instance_id.as_str() {
                return Err(...);
            }
        }
        // <-- falls through with no error if inst_val is non-Text
    }

`Value::as_text()` returns `None` for any variant other than
`Value::Text`. So a token whose INSTANCE_ID claim was emitted as
`Integer`, `Bytes`, `Array`, `Map`, `Bool`, `Null`, `Float`, or `Tag`
got past the binding check entirely — the verifier accepted it as if
no instance was declared. That is the standard CBOR type-confusion
pattern: the spec assumes Text and the receiver elides the type
guard.

The legitimate builder paths only ever emit Text:

* `CwtClaims::target_instance(&str)` constructs `Value::Text`.
* `CapabilityTokenBuilder::target_instance(&str)` delegates to it.

So a non-Text INSTANCE_ID is either a malformed token from a buggy
issuer or a deliberately-crafted token from an attacker that wants to
replay an instance-bound capability against an unrelated instance.
Either way, fail closed: a non-Text INSTANCE_ID is treated as a
malformed claim, not as "no binding declared".

Sibling check at step 3 (zone binding, lines 1423-1435) already gets
this right via `claims.get_zone_id()`, which filters to Text and
returns None for any other variant — and the verify path then routes
None into a `MissingField { field: "iss_zone" }` error. This change
brings instance binding to the same standard.

Fix:

    if let Some(inst_val) = claims.get(fcp2_claims::INSTANCE_ID) {
        let inst_str = inst_val.as_text().ok_or_else(|| FcpError::MissingField {
            field: "instance_id (must be CBOR text)".into(),
        })?;
        if inst_str != self.instance_id.as_str() {
            return Err(FcpError::ZoneViolation { ... });
        }
    }

Existing variant `FcpError::MissingField` carries the failure — no
new error variant required, no error-numbering churn. The
`(must be CBOR text)` parenthetical in the field name is the
operator-grep handle for future log triage.

Inline comment in `verify` records the trap so the next reader does
not re-introduce the silent fallthrough while "tightening up" the
nested let chain.

Two regression tests added:

* `verify_rejects_non_text_instance_id_claim`: builds a token via
  `CwtClaims::custom(INSTANCE_ID, Value::Integer(0))` (the only
  builder path that lets the test inject a non-Text claim, since the
  typed builders all funnel through Text). Asserts the verifier
  returns `MissingField` with "instance_id" in the message. Pre-fix
  this test would pass `verifier.verify(...)` cleanly — the worst
  outcome since the entire instance binding is silently disabled.
* `verify_accepts_text_instance_id_claim_when_matching`: companion
  test that exercises the happy path with a properly-typed Text
  INSTANCE_ID matching the verifier's configured instance_id.
  Establishes that the fix did not over-tighten the gate; legitimate
  instance-bound tokens still verify.

Verified locally with rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc1:
* `cargo test -p fcp-core --lib` → 3915 passed (+2 new), 0 failed
* `cargo clippy -p fcp-core --lib -- -D warnings` → clean

Audit also examined and noted (not patched in this commit, scope/
breaking-change reasons):

* Delegation depth (`fcp2_claims::DELEGATION_DEPTH`,
  `fcp2_claims::PARENT_TOKEN`) is set on tokens at issuance
  (`crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3755-3756, 3922-3928`) and
  surfaced via inspection responses, but `Grep` confirms no code in
  fcp-core, fcp-host, or anywhere else actually reads
  `delegation_depth` and rejects a token that exceeds the granted
  ceiling. The field is informational only today. Filing as a
  follow-up: until delegation enforcement lands, callers should treat
  `max_delegation_depth` as a documentation artifact rather than a
  security boundary.
* `ManagedConnectorConfig` has no `zone_id` field
  (`crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:33-59`), so the host has no
  static binding between a connector and the zone it is allowed to
  serve. The zone is supplied per-request via `request.zone_id`, and
  `ensure_handshake` (line 198) re-handshakes the connector for any
  new zone. The single-zone-per-connector rule from AGENTS.md is
  therefore enforced only by the connector's own handshake response;
  if the connector doesn't reject zones outside its intended binding,
  a token for one zone could drive an operation against a connector
  configured for another. Out of scope here — a real fix needs a
  config-schema addition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
